data.title: Frederik Meijer Gardens announces 2026 events with Chihuly exhibition, Jaume Plensa retrospective, third year of ENLIGHTEN.
data.description: Grand Rapids, Michigan - On January 9, 2026, Frederik Meijer Gardens & Sculpture Park in Grand Rapids showcased the comeback of Dale Chihuly along with the continuous Jaume Plensa retrospective. The third annual ENLIGHTEN also kicked off, offering signature horticulture and cultural experiences to visitors. This event marks a significant year for the park, attracting numerous attendees.

data.title: Bitcoin could potentially reach $53M per coin by 2050, as per VanEck's latest bullish prediction.
data.description: Is it possible that Bitcoin could surge in value to over $50 million per coin within the next 24 years? VanEck has recently released its most optimistic price forecast, suggesting a potential tremendous growth in the cryptocurrency.

data.title: BLS has 25% staff decrease and 40% leadership vacancies post 2025 shutdown, raising concerns over US economic data quality.
data.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) is facing a staffing crisis as confirmed by the BLS press office, with a reduction of about 25% in agency staffing over the past year. Currently, approximately 40% of leadership positions within the BLS are vacant. Concerns have been raised by economists, including former BLS Commissioner William Beach, who highlighted at the 2026 ASSA conference that the Department of Labor leadership appears unsupportive of the Bureau. Market skeptics like economist David Rosenberg have long suspected a deterioration in the quality of government data post the federal shutdown in late 2025. These revelations emphasize the significance of the situation and the impact it may have on the reliability of economic data going forward.

data.title: RoboCT showcases integrated ecosystem strategy at CES 2026, expanding into consumer market with exoskeletons as 'new organs'.
data.description: Using its expertise in medical robotics, a company is venturing into the consumer market and introducing exoskeletons as essential 'new organs'. Recently showcased at CES 2026 in Las Vegas, RoboCT's ambitious plan goes beyond individual products. The Chinese company is expanding its reach, aiming to revolutionize the industry.

data.title: Stocks in homebuilding and mortgage industry groups surged, as Trump aims for lower mortgage rates.
data.description: On Friday morning, Investor's Business Daily reported that the homebuilding and mortgage industry groups of IBD were among the top 20 out of 197 groups. This ranking followed President Trump's push for lower mortgage rates. The groups' strong performance indicates investor confidence in the sectors, with significant gains seen in both categories. This development reflects positive market sentiment and potential opportunities for investors in the homebuilding and mortgage industries as a result of the focus on reducing mortgage rates.

data.title: Humanoid robots steal the show at CES 2026, Mobileye and Nvidia in the spotlight. Crowd captivated.
data.description: At the CES 2026 technology trade show, humanoid robots sparked significant interest among attendees. Mobileye and Nvidia were particularly noteworthy companies in this conversation. The event witnessed a surge in enthusiasm for the advancements in robotics technology. The focus on humanoid robots indicated a shift towards innovation and cutting-edge developments in the industry. The buzz around these robots captured the attention of many individuals, highlighting the potential for growth and expansion in this sector. The CES 2026 showcased the immense potential and the growing importance of humanoid robots in the tech world.

data.title: Stock market hits highs with Dow, S&P 500, and Russell 2000 all rising. Nvidia, AMD stocks fall.
data.description: This week, the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Russell 2000 all reached record highs in the stock market. Despite announcing new AI chips at CES, Nvidia and AMD saw declines in their stock prices. Overall, the stock market rallied broadly, with a focus on companies like Nvidia, AMD, and Bloom Energy.

data.title: Proposed US bill seeks to outlaw prediction bets following $400,000 Polymarket win on Maduro's removal. Insider trading fears sparked legislation.
data.description: Following an incident where a Polymarket user made over $400,000 on a contract regarding the ousting of former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, a new bill has been introduced. This event raised fears of insider trading and prompted the proposal of the legislation.
